stories.title: PTY backend in pure Rust (openpty + setsid + TIOCSCTTY)
stories.verifiedBy: codex round-1 17-19-21-298Z REJECT (6 safety bugs) → fix fb97d15 → round-2 17-29-02-836Z REVISE (putenv/execvp + E0597) → fix d9bf0d4 → round-3 17-37-08-437Z CODEX_PASS. cargo test 2/2 PASS. AS-safe execve, FD_CLOEXEC, kill timeout+SIGKILL, Drop impl, JNI null guards, signature locked.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/android-host/src/pty.rs exists with public functions: spawn_pty(cmd: &str, env: &[(&str,&str)]) -> Result<PtySession>, write(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<usize>, read(buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize>, resize(rows: u16, cols: u16) -> Result<()>, kill() -> Result<()>
spawn_pty internally calls libc::openpty, fork, setsid in child, TIOCSCTTY on slave fd, dup2 to stdin/stdout/stderr, execvp
Rust unit test on host (linux/mac aarch64 if possible) spawn_pty("echo hello") returns the bytes "hello\n" via read(); test gated under #[cfg(unix)]
No dependency on Termux executables or files; pure libc + nix
PTY module exports JNI binding so Java can call Java_dev_warp_mobile_NativeBridge_ptySpawn / ptyRead / ptyWrite / ptyResize / ptyKill

stories.title: Android Service + AndroidManifest + FGS skeleton

minSdkVersion 31, targetSdk 36, compileSdk 36 in build.gradle
Service loads libwarp_mobile_android_host.so via System.loadLibrary
adb install + launch on S24 Ultra shows the persistent notification visible
adb shell dumpsys activity services dev.warp.mobile lists the Service in foreground state

stories.title: Activity recreate -> PTY reattach within 1s
stories.structuralPassNote: see top-level note — drivers structurally PASS round-5; device run pending Task #32
acceptanceCriteria: Service holds the PTY session (Service-process-bound) so it survives Activity destroy/recreate
Activity binds to Service via ServiceConnection on each onCreate, gets a reference to the existing PtySession

Acceptance test on S24 Ultra: spawn `sleep 60 && echo done`, rotate device 5 times, then wait. Activity prints `done` within 1s of the actual fire (verify via adb shell logcat WarpTerminal:I time-tagging)
Test driver script under tools/scripts/test-pty-reattach.sh + artifact under .omc/m1-artifacts/ documenting run

stories.title: PTY resize via TIOCSWINSZ -> stty size in shell
stories.structuralPassNote: see top-level note — drivers structurally PASS round-5; device run pending Task #32
acceptanceCriteria: Java side reads view dims and calls NativeBridge.ptyResize(cols, rows) on layout change
Rust resize() calls libc::ioctl(fd, TIOCSWINSZ, &winsize{rows, cols, 0, 0})
Acceptance test on S24 Ultra: spawn `stty size > /tmp/sz; cat /tmp/sz` after resize(80, 24); the file content equals "24 80\n"
Artifact under .omc/m1-artifacts/ with adb logcat evidence

stories.title: FGS persistence + clean kill
stories.structuralPassNote: see top-level note — drivers structurally PASS round-5; device run pending Task #32
acceptanceCriteria: Notification visible continuously during PTY session
adb shell am kill dev.warp.mobile triggers Service.onDestroy which calls pty.kill() and removes notification
After kill: ps -A | grep dev.warp shows no orphan processes (especially no zombie PTY children)
Artifact under .omc/m1-artifacts/ logs the before/after process listings

stories.title: 30-min idle stress test on S24 Ultra flagship
stories.structuralPassNote: see top-level note — drivers structurally PASS round-5; device run pending Task #32
acceptanceCriteria: Spawn `bash` PTY session, leave idle 30 minutes on S24 Ultra
No crashes (no app death, no Service termination)
No PhantomProcessKiller events in logcat
Notification still present at t=30min
PTY still responsive: write `pwd\n` returns the cwd in <500ms
Artifact .omc/m1-artifacts/M1-stress-test.md documenting run with raw logcat snippets at t=0/10/20/30 min
